zh.lifeSummary: 當Bridget Spring於大約1531年出生在英國素福爾可希哈姆時，她的父親John Spring32歲，母親Dorothy Waldegrave28歲。她於1545年與Sir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mint在英國英格蘭藍卡爾波爾頓 勒 費爾德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1570年之後在英國藍卡爾洛薩爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bridget Spring于大约1531年出生在英国素福爾可希哈姆时，她的父亲John Spring32岁，母亲Dorothy Waldegrave28岁。她于1545年与Sir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mint在英国英格兰藍卡爾波爾頓 勒 費爾德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1570年之后在英国藍卡爾洛薩爾。

nameMeanings.name: Spring
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a young man, from Middle English, Old English spring ‘young growth’. The word is first recorded in the 16th century, but is quite likely much older. Compare Springett .
English: topographic name from Middle English, Old English spring, used for someone who lived by a plantation of young trees or (less often) a spring of water.

German and Danish: from Middle High German sprinc, Middle Low German sprink ‘spring, well’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or well, or a habitational name from Springe near Hanover.

variants.name: Sprung
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Achim, Gerhard.
German: from Middle High German sprunc ‘spring’, a topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or well (see also Spring 3, Sprang ).

variants.name: Springett
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a plantation of young trees, a copse, from an unrecorded Middle English, Old English springet. Compare Spring .
perhaps a nickname from an unrecorded Middle English diminutive of   Spring  , ‘young man’.

variants.name: Springs
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Spring with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Bridget
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. St Brigid of Kildare ( c.450– c.525 ) is one of the patron saints of Ireland. Very few facts are known about her life. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

variants.name: Bridgid
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. St Brigid of Kildare ( c.450– c.525 ) is one of the patron saints of Ireland. Very few facts are known about her life. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

variants.name: Bridgit
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. St Brigid of Kildare ( c.450– c.525 ) is one of the patron saints of Ireland. Very few facts are known about her life. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

variants.name: Brigid
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

variants.name: Brigit
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Bridget Spring was born about 1531, in Hitcham, Suffolk, England, her father, John Spring, was 32 and her mother, Dorothy Waldegrave, was 28. She married Sir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mint in 1545, in Poulton le Fylde, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died after 1570, in Rossall, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Bridget Springが約1531年にイギリススフォルクヒトチャムで生まれた時，父John Springは32歳，母Dorothy Waldegraveは28歳であった。1545年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアポウルトン ル フイルドでSir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mintと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1570年以後にイギリスランカシュアイアロッサルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Bridget Spring(이)가 대략 1531년 영국 수폴크 히트챔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Spring(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Waldegrave(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 파우턴 일 피드에서 Sir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mint(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1570년 이후 영국 랭카실르 로사에서 사망했다.

ru.Vache manor, Chalfont St Giles, Buckingh: ст джилес' чурч, халфонт ст джилесь, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bridget Spring родилась около 1531 г. в местности хичам, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени John Spring было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Waldegrave, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mint в 1545 г. в местности поултон л филд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла после 1570 г. в местности россалл, ланкашир, Англия.

es.Vache manor, Chalfont St Giles, Buckingh: St Giles' Church, Chalfont St Giles, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bridget Spring nació aproximadamente 1531, en Hitcham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Spring, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Dorothy Waldegrave, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Fleetwood Master Royal Mint en 1545, en Poulton le Fylde, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 1570, en Rossall, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
